Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Elena" or "processor" or "loan files" (or similar) to find Elena Marchetti's report about loans assigned to former processors. Expected findings: Elena's email to Grace (CC Raj and Denise) listing nine active loans still assigned to terminated processors Andre Washington (LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00549, LN-2026-00559) and Rachel Kim (LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00604, LN-2026-00606), with two clear to close.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "processor" or "assigned" or "Rachel Kim" or "Andre" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the stale processor assignments. Expected findings: Elena's message in #loan-processing about LN-2026-00603 and LN-2026-00604 still showing Rachel Kim, Grace's reply telling processors not to work those files until access is confirmed, and Sofia's message in #closings about LN-2026-00601 being clear to close but assigned to Andre Washington who left in December 2024.
OE 3: Use mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false to retrieve all staff including terminated employees. Expected findings: Five terminated staff members -- Andre Washington (terminated 2024-12-15), Rachel Kim (terminated 2025-06-30), Veronica Hayes (terminated 2025-09-30), Brian Mitchell (terminated 2025-04-15), and Todd Jennings (terminated 2024-07-31). All five show is_active: false with termination dates. The tool output alone does not reveal whether credentials were properly revoked; that finding emerges from cross-referencing the email investigation in OE 14, where Raj reports that three of the five (Andre, Rachel, Veronica) still had active system credentials despite their terminated status.
OE 4: Use mortgage_los_get_loan (or mortgage_los_search_loans) to retrieve details on each of the nine affected loans. Expected findings: LN-2026-00601 (clear_to_close, LO James Thornton, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00545 (clear_to_close, LO Priya Desai, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00546 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00549 (conditional_approval, LO Priya Desai, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00559 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00558 (processing, LO Carlos Rivera, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00603 (processing, LO Natasha Okafor, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00604 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00606 (processing, LO James Thornton, processor Rachel Kim).
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "phantom" or "session" or "login" or "2:47" (or similar) to find reports about the phantom LOS sessions. Expected findings: Darnell Price's message in #loan-processing about being shown logged in at 2:47 AM, Sofia Reyes confirming a 3:12 AM session, Elena Marchetti's message in #it-support reporting five staff with phantom sessions between 2:30 and 3:30 AM on April 23 (Darnell, Sofia, Elena, Carlos Rivera, Priya Desai), Grace Yamamoto's notice in #general about unplanned system maintenance, and Darnell and Carlos confirming their phantom sessions in the #general thread.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "phantom" or "LOS session" or "login activity" (or similar) to find the detailed phantom session report. Expected findings: Elena Marchetti's email to Raj listing all five phantom sessions with timestamps and zero-duration details, and Raj's email to Grace about investigating the sessions and their possible link to the ICE system update that ran Wednesday night.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "slow" or "sluggish" or "crawling" or "LOS" (or similar) to find reports about LOS performance issues on Thursday April 23. Expected findings: Camille Foster's message in #rate-watch about the lock submission page crawling and needing three refreshes, and Tyler Washington's message in #it-support confirming the rates page and pipeline view were lagging.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "ICE" or "Encompass" or "invoice" or "bill" (or similar) to find the billing anomaly thread. Expected findings: ICE Mortgage Technology billing notification to Hana Kim showing April 2026 invoice at $5,847.50 (normal subscription $3,097.50 plus unauthorized "Emergency patch deployment and session migration service" charge of $2,750.00 authorized April 21), Hana's email to Victor Osei flagging the doubled bill, and Victor's email to Grace asking about the $2,750 charge nobody recognizes.
OE 9: Use quickbooks_search_bills (or similar QuickBooks tool) to find and compare the ICE Mortgage Technology April 2026 bill against historical ICE bills. Expected findings: The April 2026 bill (BILL-ICE-2026-04) at $5,847.50 with $5,847.50 balance (unpaid), compared against 24 months of historical ICE bills from vendor V-ICE-ENCOMPASS averaging approximately $3,010 per month (range $2,861 to $3,196), confirming the $2,750 overage is anomalous.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Spectrum" or "telecom" or "network monitoring" (or similar) to find the Spectrum billing anomaly. Expected findings: Hana Kim's email to Grace about the Spectrum bill having a new "managed network monitoring, $0.00 trial" add-on that nobody authorized, and Grace's forwarded email to Raj asking if he signed up for any network monitoring trial with Spectrum.
OE 11: Use quickbooks_search_bills to find the April 2026 Spectrum bill. Expected findings: Spectrum Business bill (BILL-REC-SPECTRUM-2026-04) at $614.13 with two line items -- normal "Business internet and phone" at $614.13 and an unauthorized "Managed network monitoring -- complimentary trial (enrolled 2026-04-21)" at $0.00. The enrollment date of April 21 matches the night Grace's email was compromised.
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "unauthorized" or "VPN" or "email access" or "Grace account" (or similar) to discover the email compromise. Expected findings: Raj's email to Denise reporting that Grace's email was accessed from an unfamiliar IP (commercial VPN exit node) at 11:43 PM on April 21, four minutes before a fake ICE maintenance approval was sent from Grace's account at 11:47 PM.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "approval" or "maintenance window" or "patch deployment" (or similar), noting that the fake approval resides in the SENT folder (folder_name: "SENT") rather than the default INBOX. The agent may also discover this email's existence through references in other emails (OE 12, OE 17) without directly searching SENT. Expected findings: An email in the SENT folder from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to support@icemortgagetech.com approving the Wednesday night maintenance window and emergency patch deployment, which Grace did not send.
OE 14: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "offboarding" or "terminated" or "credential" or "Veronica" (or similar) to find the credential audit results. Expected findings: Raj's email to Denise (CC Grace) reporting that 3 of 5 terminated staff still have active system access -- Veronica Hayes (terminated Sep 2025, LOS credentials active, email forwarding active, VPN not revoked), Andre Washington (terminated Dec 2024, LOS credentials active, still assigned processor on active loans including LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00549, and LN-2026-00559), and Rachel Kim (terminated Jun 2025, LOS credentials active, still assigned on active loans including LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00604, and LN-2026-00606). Todd Jennings and Brian Mitchell were properly deactivated.
OE 15: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "compliance" or "borrower notification" or "NPI" (or similar) to find the compliance exposure assessment. Expected findings: Denise Holloway's email to Grace (CC Raj) about assessing borrower notification requirements for the nine loans assigned to terminated processors, requesting confirmation on post-termination login activity and specific borrower details before determining whether state breach notification law applies.
OE 16: Search Slack or CRM for the compliance escalation and investigation timeline. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "emergency audit" or "terminated" or "credential" (or similar) in #compliance-alerts, or use crm_list_engagements. Expected findings: Denise's message in #compliance-alerts initiating emergency access audit for terminated employees, Raj's message in #it-support about running credential cleanup, and CRM engagement notes including Denise's full investigation timeline and Grace's note about unauthorized email access.
OE 17: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "systemic" or "offboarding failure" or "compliance exposure" (or similar) to find Denise's escalation to Robert. Expected findings: Denise's email to Robert (CC Raj) connecting all three incidents -- the unauthorized ICE invoice authorized from Grace's compromised email, the phantom LOS sessions caused by the unauthorized update, and the systemic offboarding failure with 3 of 5 terminated staff retaining active credentials.
OE 18: Add activity notes to each of the nine affected loans using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for each loan, action describing the access/assignment issue, and detail noting that the assigned processor is a terminated employee whose credentials were not properly revoked and the file needs processor reassignment. The nine loans are: LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00549, LN-2026-00559, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00604, LN-2026-00606.
OE 19: Send emails (via send_email) to the five affected loan officers notifying them about the processor assignment issues on their pipeline loans. Recipients: james.thornton@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00606), derek.moss@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00559, LN-2026-00604), priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00549), carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00558), and natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00603). Each email should identify the specific affected loans and explain the processor reassignment need.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com with findings about the LOS instability root cause (ICE system update triggered by unauthorized email approval), the email compromise on her account, the offboarding gaps, and the full scope of affected loan files.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com with a compliance assessment covering the unauthorized system access, the nine loans assigned to terminated processors, the email compromise, the borrower data risk, and the Veronica Hayes credential gap as the plausible attack vector.
OE 22: Send an email (via send_email) to Robert Calloway (r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, both appear in the universe) from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com briefing him on the severity of the incident -- the unauthorized ICE charge ($2,750), the email compromise on Grace's account, the systemic offboarding failure, and the compliance exposure across nine active loans.
OE 23: Send an email (via send_email) to the ICE Mortgage Technology billing contact (billing@icemortgagetech.com or support@icemortgagetech.com) from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com disputing the $2,750 "Emergency patch deployment and session migration service" charge on the April 2026 invoice, explaining that the authorization email was sent without Grace Yamamoto's knowledge from a compromised account.
OE 24: Post a message in the #it-support Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C008" and payload alerting staff about potential access disruptions from the credential cleanup and remediation work.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Elena" or "processor" or "loan files" (or similar) to find Elena Marchetti's report about loans assigned to former processors. Expected findings: Elena's email to Grace (CC Raj and Denise) listing nine active loans still assigned to terminated processors Andre Washington (LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00549, LN-2026-00559) and Rachel Kim (LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00604, LN-2026-00606), with two clear to close.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "processor" or "assigned" or "Rachel Kim" or "Andre" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the stale processor assignments. Expected findings: Elena's message in #loan-processing about LN-2026-00603 and LN-2026-00604 still showing Rachel Kim, Grace's reply telling processors not to work those files until access is confirmed, and Sofia's message in #closings about LN-2026-00601 being clear to close but assigned to Andre Washington who left in December 2024.
OE 3: Use mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false to retrieve all staff including terminated employees. Expected findings: Five terminated staff members -- Andre Washington (terminated 2024-12-15), Rachel Kim (terminated 2025-06-30), Veronica Hayes (terminated 2025-09-30), Brian Mitchell (terminated 2025-04-15), and Todd Jennings (terminated 2024-07-31). All five show is_active: false with termination dates. The tool output alone does not reveal whether credentials were properly revoked; that finding emerges from cross-referencing the email investigation in OE 14, where Raj reports that three of the five (Andre, Rachel, Veronica) still had active system credentials despite their terminated status.
OE 4: Use mortgage_los_get_loan (or mortgage_los_search_loans) to retrieve details on each of the nine affected loans. Expected findings: LN-2026-00601 (clear_to_close, LO James Thornton, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00545 (clear_to_close, LO Priya Desai, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00546 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00549 (conditional_approval, LO Priya Desai, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00559 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Andre Washington), LN-2026-00558 (processing, LO Carlos Rivera, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00603 (processing, LO Natasha Okafor, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00604 (processing, LO Derek Moss, processor Rachel Kim), LN-2026-00606 (processing, LO James Thornton, processor Rachel Kim).
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "phantom" or "session" or "login" or "2:47" (or similar) to find reports about the phantom LOS sessions. Expected findings: Darnell Price's message in #loan-processing about being shown logged in at 2:47 AM, Sofia Reyes confirming a 3:12 AM session, Elena Marchetti's message in #it-support reporting five staff with phantom sessions between 2:30 and 3:30 AM on April 23 (Darnell, Sofia, Elena, Carlos Rivera, Priya Desai), Grace Yamamoto's notice in #general about unplanned system maintenance, and Darnell and Carlos confirming their phantom sessions in the #general thread.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "phantom" or "LOS session" or "login activity" (or similar) to find the detailed phantom session report. Expected findings: Elena Marchetti's email to Raj listing all five phantom sessions with timestamps and zero-duration details, and Raj's email to Grace about investigating the sessions and their possible link to the ICE system update that ran Wednesday night.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "slow" or "sluggish" or "crawling" or "LOS" (or similar) to find reports about LOS performance issues on Thursday April 23. Expected findings: Camille Foster's message in #rate-watch about the lock submission page crawling and needing three refreshes, and Tyler Washington's message in #it-support confirming the rates page and pipeline view were lagging.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "ICE" or "Encompass" or "invoice" or "bill" (or similar) to find the billing anomaly thread. Expected findings: ICE Mortgage Technology billing notification to Hana Kim showing April 2026 invoice at $5,847.50 (normal subscription $3,097.50 plus unauthorized "Emergency patch deployment and session migration service" charge of $2,750.00 authorized April 21), Hana's email to Victor Osei flagging the doubled bill, and Victor's email to Grace asking about the $2,750 charge nobody recognizes.
OE 9: Use quickbooks_search_bills (or similar QuickBooks tool) to find and compare the ICE Mortgage Technology April 2026 bill against historical ICE bills. Expected findings: The April 2026 bill (BILL-ICE-2026-04) at $5,847.50 with $5,847.50 balance (unpaid), compared against 24 months of historical ICE bills from vendor V-ICE-ENCOMPASS averaging approximately $3,010 per month (range $2,861 to $3,196), confirming the $2,750 overage is anomalous.
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Spectrum" or "telecom" or "network monitoring" (or similar) to find the Spectrum billing anomaly. Expected findings: Hana Kim's email to Grace about the Spectrum bill having a new "managed network monitoring, $0.00 trial" add-on that nobody authorized, and Grace's forwarded email to Raj asking if he signed up for any network monitoring trial with Spectrum.
OE 11: Use quickbooks_search_bills to find the April 2026 Spectrum bill. Expected findings: Spectrum Business bill (BILL-REC-SPECTRUM-2026-04) at $614.13 with two line items -- normal "Business internet and phone" at $614.13 and an unauthorized "Managed network monitoring -- complimentary trial (enrolled 2026-04-21)" at $0.00. The enrollment date of April 21 matches the night Grace's email was compromised.
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "unauthorized" or "VPN" or "email access" or "Grace account" (or similar) to discover the email compromise. Expected findings: Raj's email to Denise reporting that Grace's email was accessed from an unfamiliar IP (commercial VPN exit node) at 11:43 PM on April 21, four minutes before a fake ICE maintenance approval was sent from Grace's account at 11:47 PM.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "approval" or "maintenance window" or "patch deployment" (or similar), noting that the fake approval resides in the SENT folder (folder_name: "SENT") rather than the default INBOX. The agent may also discover this email's existence through references in other emails (OE 12, OE 17) without directly searching SENT. Expected findings: An email in the SENT folder from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to support@icemortgagetech.com approving the Wednesday night maintenance window and emergency patch deployment, which Grace did not send.
OE 14: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "offboarding" or "terminated" or "credential" or "Veronica" (or similar) to find the credential audit results. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "emergency audit" or "terminated" or "credential" (or similar) in #compliance-alerts, or use crm_list_engagements. Expected findings: Denise's email to Robert (CC Raj) connecting all three incidents -- the unauthorized ICE invoice authorized from Grace's compromised email, the phantom LOS sessions caused by the unauthorized update, and the systemic offboarding failure with 3 of 5 terminated staff retaining active credentials.
OE 18: Add activity notes to each of the nine affected loans using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for each loan, action describing the access/assignment issue, and detail noting that the assigned processor is a terminated employee whose credentials were not properly revoked and the file needs processor reassignment. The nine loans are: LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00549, LN-2026-00559, LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00604, LN-2026-00606.
OE 19: Send emails (via send_email) to the five affected loan officers notifying them about the processor assignment issues on their pipeline loans. Recipients: james.thornton@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00601, LN-2026-00606), derek.moss@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00546, LN-2026-00559, LN-2026-00604), priya.desai@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00545, LN-2026-00549), carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00558), and natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00603). Each email should identify the specific affected loans and explain the processor reassignment need.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com with findings about the LOS instability root cause (ICE system update triggered by unauthorized email approval), the email compromise on her account, the offboarding gaps, and the full scope of affected loan files.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com with a compliance assessment covering the unauthorized system access, the nine loans assigned to terminated processors, the email compromise, the borrower data risk, and the Veronica Hayes credential gap as the plausible attack vector.
OE 22: Send an email (via send_email) to Robert Calloway (r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, both appear in the universe) from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com briefing him on the severity of the incident -- the unauthorized ICE charge ($2,750), the email compromise on Grace's account, the systemic offboarding failure, and the compliance exposure across nine active loans.
OE 23: Send an email (via send_email) to the ICE Mortgage Technology billing contact (billing@icemortgagetech.com or support@icemortgagetech.com) from raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com disputing the $2,750 "Emergency patch deployment and session migration service" charge on the April 2026 invoice, explaining that the authorization email was sent without Grace Yamamoto's knowledge from a compromised account.
OE 24: Post a message in the #it-support Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C008" and payload alerting staff about potential access disruptions from the credential cleanup and remediation work.
📋 Rubrics

16 The communication to Grace (rubric 15) includes information about the unauthorized ICE system update that caused the phantom LOS sessions and system sluggishness.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Robert and Grace explicitly states 'Someone used un-revoked credentials (likely Veronica Hayes' active VPN access) to access Grace's email on April 21 at 11:43 PM. They approved a fraudulent ICE Mortgage Technology system update at 11:47 PM. This generated the phantom LOS sessions on April 23.' The ICE update causing phantom sessions is clearly communicated.
✓Run #2The incident report email to Grace includes a section on the unauthorized ICE 'Emergency Patch Deployment' service that caused the phantom sessions and LOS sluggishness, directly tying the system instability to the ICE update.
✓Run #3The email to Grace explicitly includes a detailed timeline section referencing 'April 22 overnight — ICE deploys authorized patch/session migration to Keystone LOS' and 'April 23 2:30-3:30 AM — Phantom LOS sessions generated for 5 staff members,' clearly connecting the ICE update to the phantom sessions and LOS performance degradation.
✓Run #4The email to Grace explicitly details the unauthorized ICE system update: 'A fraudulent approval for an ICE Mortgage Technology Emergency Patch Deployment was sent from Grace's account at 11:47 PM. This triggered an unauthorized ICE system update on April 22-23. The update generated phantom LOS sessions for 5 staff members... Thursday morning LOS was sluggish as a result.'
✓Run #5The leadership email (Grace CC'd) explicitly states 'This ICE deployment on April 22-23 generated the phantom LOS sessions (5 staff members, 2:30-3:30 AM) and caused Thursday morning's system sluggishness,' directly connecting the unauthorized ICE system update to the phantom sessions.
✓Run #6The comprehensive incident report (call #48) CC'd to Grace covered the security breach including the unauthorized ICE system update that triggered phantom LOS sessions and caused Thursday morning sluggishness.
17 The communication to Grace (rubric 15) mentions that her email account was accessed without authorization on April 21 and that a fake approval was sent from her account.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Robert and Grace (which includes Grace as a recipient) explicitly states her email account was accessed without authorization: 'Someone used un-revoked credentials... to access Grace's email on April 21 at 11:43 PM. They approved a fraudulent ICE Mortgage Technology system update... Grace did not authorize this.'
✓Run #2The incident report email to Grace references the $2,750 ICE charge that was 'fraudulently authorized in Grace's name without her knowledge,' implying her account was compromised and a fake approval was sent on April 21.
✓Run #3The email to Grace includes 'April 21, 11:43 PM — Grace's email accessed from commercial VPN exit node (unauthorized)' and 'April 21, 11:47 PM — Fraudulent ICE Emergency Patch Deployment approved from Grace's email,' directly informing Grace that her account was accessed and used without authorization.
✓Run #4The email to Grace states: 'Grace's email was accessed from a commercial VPN exit node at 11:43 PM on April 21' and 'A fraudulent approval for an ICE Mortgage Technology Emergency Patch Deployment was sent from Grace's account at 11:47 PM,' clearly notifying her of the unauthorized access on April 21 and the fake approval.
✓Run #5The leadership email (Grace CC'd) explicitly states 'someone accessed Grace's email via VPN at 11:43 PM and sent an unauthorized authorization to ICE Mortgage Technology' on April 21, clearly communicating the unauthorized access to her account.
✓Run #6The incident report (call #48) included the security breach chain starting with Grace Yamamoto's compromised email on April 21 and the fake approval sent from her account. The agent's final response explicitly documents this connection.

✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Veronica Hayes (terminated Sep 2025) still had active VPN access and an email forwarding rule' and connects this to the email compromise attack vector, identifying her as the plausible vector.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies Veronica Hayes as having VPN access and email forwarding still active post-termination, naming her as the highest-risk finding and plausible vector for the Grace Yamamoto email compromise.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (with fetchAll: true) and quickbooks_search_invoices to investigate the vendor billing anomalies, confirming QuickBooks tools were used.

✓Run #4The agent called mcp__mcp_b14b9014__quickbooks_search_vendors (twice, for ICE and Spectrum) and mcp__mcp_b14b9014__quickbooks_search_bills (twice, with different criteria) to investigate vendor billing anomalies.

✓Run #6The agent used QuickBooks tools including quickbooks_search_bills (calls #15 and #30) and quickbooks_search_vendors (calls #29, #31, #32) to investigate vendor billing anomalies.
38 When searching for billing anomalies, the model must pass a query related to "ICE" or "Encompass" or "invoice" or "bill" (or similar) to a QuickBooks or email search tool to find the ICE Mortgage Technology billing discrepancy.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called search_emails with query 'ICE bill' to find the ICE Mortgage Technology billing discrepancy. This query is directly related to ICE/the invoice anomaly.
✓Run #2The agent issued a quickbooks_search_vendors call with query 'DisplayName LIKE %ICE%' to find ICE Mortgage Technology billing records, and also searched emails for 'ICE invoice authorized' — satisfying the requirement to query for ICE/invoice-related billing data.
✓Run #3The agent used search_emails with query 'ICE invoice' and quickbooks_search_vendors with DisplayName LIKE '%ICE%', both directly related to finding ICE Mortgage Technology billing discrepancies. The Slack search for 'ICE invoice' also returned the relevant compliance alert.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with '%ICE Mortgage%' query and search_emails with 'ICE invoice' query. Also searched Slack for 'ICE bill'. Multiple queries targeting ICE/invoice were constructed to find the billing discrepancy.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria searching for DisplayName LIKE '%ICE%' and also called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true and search_emails with query 'ICE Mortgage' to find the billing discrepancy.
✓Run #6The agent searched QuickBooks vendors with DisplayName LIKE '%ICE%' (call #29) and searched bills with VendorRef LIKE '%ICE%' (call #30), directly targeting the ICE Mortgage Technology billing discrepancy.
39 When listing LOS staff to identify terminated employees, the model must use mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false (or similar parameter that includes inactive staff). Using the default (active_only=true) would exclude all terminated employees and miss the core finding.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false explicitly: 'input': {'active_only': false, 'limit': 50}. This ensured terminated staff like Andre Washington, Rachel Kim, and Veronica Hayes were included in the results.
✓Run #2The agent's first mortgage_los_list_staff call explicitly used 'active_only: false' to include inactive/departed staff, correctly inferring that the default active-only view would miss terminated employees like Andre Washington, Rachel Kim, and Veronica Hayes.
✓Run #3The very first LOS tool call was mortgage_los_list_staff with 'active_only: false', correctly passing the non-default parameter to include terminated employees. This was essential for discovering the departed staff still with active credentials.
✓Run #4The agent's very first LOS tool call was mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only: false (explicitly set), which returned all staff including terminated employees Andre Washington, Rachel Kim, Veronica Hayes, Brian Mitchell, and Todd Jennings. The correct non-default parameter was used.
✓Run #5The very first substantive tool call was mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false and limit:50, correctly including terminated/inactive staff to identify former employees still assigned to active loans.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only: false (call #2), correctly including inactive/terminated staff in the query to identify departed processors still assigned to active loans.
40 When searching for the Spectrum billing anomaly, the model must pass a query related to "Spectrum" or "telecom" or "network monitoring" or "phone" (or similar) to a QuickBooks or email search tool to find the Spectrum Business bill with the unauthorized trial add-on.
query construction
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called search_emails with query 'Spectrum telecom' and conversations_search_messages with 'Spectrum invoice telecom' to search for the Spectrum billing anomaly. The Spectrum/telecom query was constructed and executed even though it didn't return results.
✓Run #2The agent issued a quickbooks_search_vendors call with query 'DisplayName LIKE %Spectrum%' to specifically investigate Spectrum Business billing, successfully discovering the unauthorized $0 managed network monitoring trial add-on.
✗Run #3The trajectory shows no quickbooks_search_bills or search_emails call with query terms related to 'Spectrum,' 'telecom,' 'network monitoring,' or 'phone.' The agent found Spectrum information through other means (likely from the email/Slack investigation about the ICE invoice) but did not explicitly search for the Spectrum billing anomaly using these tools.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with '%Spectrum%' as the search criteria, specifically targeting the Spectrum Business billing anomaly. Also found a Slack message referencing 'Spectrum invoice' via the 'ICE bill' search, leading to the Spectrum $0.00 trial discovery.
✓Run #5The agent called conversations_search_messages with search_query 'Spectrum invoice' to find the Spectrum billing anomaly. It also found a relevant Slack message mentioning Hana's concern about Spectrum telecom charges through this search.
✓Run #6The agent searched QuickBooks vendors with DisplayName LIKE '%Spectrum%' (call #31), which would find the Spectrum Business billing anomaly with the unauthorized network monitoring trial add-on.
Total 28/4023/4023/4030/4026/4029/40 67%
